title: Daily Briefing — Jun 04
headline: Rotational shift in play: tech showing cracks, cyclicals quietly improving.
body: Right, so here's the big picture: Tech is still in the lead (XLK RS=127.2), but we're seeing bearish biases emerge in NVDA, GOOGL, AMZN, and XLK is actually down -2.4% today. The VIX is holding at 16.06, trending below its SMA20, signaling low volatility and complacency in the tape. Fear & Greed is neutral at 53.5 — no outright signals, but momentum is cooling off from the highs last month.

Rotation is the story: Energy (XLE), Materials (XLB), Industrials (XLI), and Healthcare (XLV) are all moving from 'elevated risk' to 'improving.' RS levels are rising, and price is climbing above the 20 and 50 MAs. DHR just triggered above its MAs, and UPS & AA broke out above all key levels — classic rotational play. Tech leadership is getting crowded, but cyclicals are quietly gaining strength beneath the surface.

Contrarian flag: NVDA, AMZN, GOOGL all sitting below their 20 MA. That triggers my technical risk rule — when leaders daily close under 20 MA, exit or reduce longs. XLF (the canary) is lagging despite a price pop, and Discretionary (XLY) remains high risk and lagging. If it's in the press, it's in the price, and right now consensus is still tech. Flows are signaling a stealth rotation — patience, don't chase.
callout_body:
NVDA: $219.26 (20 MA — below, caution zone)
AMZN: $265.39 (20 MA — below, caution zone)
XLF: $51.43 (20 MA — just above, but lagging in RRG, watch for breakdown)
DHR: $172.32 (20 MA — triggered long, relative strength pickup)
XLE: $58.12 (20 MA — holding, improving quadrant)
SPY: $745.81 (20 MA — above, bull bias intact)
action_items: Rotate watchlist toward improving sectors: XLE, XLB, XLI, XLV. Watch for confirmation above weekly 20 MA.
Reduce exposure or place stops on tech names below their 20 MA — NVDA, AMZN, GOOGL — per technical risk rule.
closing_quote: Abundance mindset: missed tech isn't a tragedy, it's a signal — find the next setup.
